# Deezul Framework

Deezul is a lightweight reactive UI framework for building web applications with Shadow DOM web components, proxy-based reactivity, and a bytecode-compiled binding system.

## Component Definition

Components are defined using `Deezul.Component()` and compiled to bytecode:

```javascript
export default Deezul.Component({
    template: `
        <div class="counter">
            <p>Count: {{ count }}</p>
            <button @click="increment">+1</button>
        </div>
    `,

    data: () => ({
        count: 0
    }),

    methods: {
        increment() {
            this.count++;
        }
    },

    computed: {
        doubled() {
            return this.count * 2;
        }
    },

    watch: {
        count(newVal, oldVal) {
            console.log(`Count changed: ${oldVal} -> ${newVal}`);
        }
    },

    styles: `
        .counter { padding: 20px; }
    `
});
```

## Template Syntax

### Text Binding
```html
<p>{{ propertyName }}</p>
<p>{{ firstName + ' ' + lastName }}</p>
```

### Two-Way Binding
```html
<input type="text" :bind="username" />
<textarea :bind="message"></textarea>
```

### Attribute Binding
```html
<div :class="activeClass"></div>
<img :src="imageUrl" />
<button :disabled="isLoading"></button>
```

### Event Handling
```html
<button @click="handleClick">Click</button>
<button @click="addItem(item)">Add</button>
<input @input="onInput" @keydown="onKey" />
```

### Conditionals
```html
<div :if="isVisible">Shown when true</div>
<div :else-if="altCondition">Alternative</div>
<div :else>Fallback</div>
```

### Loops
```html
<ul>
    <li :for="item in items">{{ item.name }}</li>
</ul>

<div :for="(item, index) in items">
    {{ index }}: {{ item }}
</div>
```

### Props (Parent to Child)
```html
<!-- Parent template -->
<dz-component dz-type="child-component" :label="parentLabel" :count.sync="parentCount"></dz-component>

<!-- Child receives props via this.label, this.count -->
<!-- .sync enables two-way prop binding -->
```

### Slots
```html
<!-- Parent -->
<dz-component dz-type="card">
    <span slot="header">Title</span>
    <p>Default slot content</p>
    <span slot="footer">Footer</span>
</dz-component>

<!-- Child template -->
<div class="card">
    <header><slot name="header"></slot></header>
    <main><slot></slot></main>
    <footer><slot name="footer"></slot></footer>
</div>
```

### Refs
```html
<input type="text" ref="myInput" />
<!-- Access via this.$refs.myInput -->
```

## Custom Directives

Register directives globally:

```javascript
Deezul.registerDirective('focus', {
    created(el, binding) { },
    mounted(el, binding) {
        if (binding.value) el.focus();
    },
    updated(el, binding) {
        if (binding.value && !binding.oldValue) el.focus();
    },
    unmounted(el, binding) { }
});
```

Use in templates:
```html
<input :focus="shouldFocus" />
```

## Lifecycle Hooks

```javascript
{
    $mounted() {
        // Component inserted into DOM
    },
    $updated() {
        // Called after any reactive update
    },
    $unmounted() {
        // Component removed from DOM
    }
}
```

## Router

Initialize with routes:

```javascript
Deezul.init({
    rootElement: 'app',

    modules: [
        { ref: 'home-page', path: '/compiled/HomePage.compiled.js' },
        { ref: 'user-page', path: '/compiled/UserPage.compiled.js' }
    ],

    routes: [
        { path: '/', component: 'home-page' },
        { path: '/users/:id', component: 'user-page' },
        { path: '/dashboard', component: 'dashboard', layouts: ['app-layout'] }
    ],

    beforeNavigate(to, from, next) {
        // Route guard - call next() to proceed
        next();
    },

    afterNavigate(to, from) {
        // Called after navigation completes
    }
});
```

Access route params:
```javascript
// In component methods/computed
this.$route.params.id
this.$route.fullPath
```

Navigate programmatically:
```javascript
this.$router.push('/users/123');
this.$router.replace('/home');
this.$router.back();
```

## Compilation

Components must be compiled before use:

```bash
node tooling/compiler/bin/cli.js src/MyComponent.js compiled/MyComponent.compiled.js
```

Watch mode:
```bash
node tooling/compiler/bin/cli.js --watch src/ --out compiled/
```

## Key Patterns

1. **Data must be a factory function**: `data: () => ({})` not `data: {}`
2. **Methods access data via `this`**: `this.count++`
3. **Computed properties are cached**: Only re-evaluate when dependencies change
4. **Watchers fire on change**: Receive `(newVal, oldVal)` arguments
5. **Styles are scoped**: Each component's styles are isolated via Shadow DOM
6. **Props flow down**: Parent binds `:propName="value"`, child receives `this.propName`
7. **Events flow up**: Child calls `this.$emit('eventName', data)`, parent handles `@eventName="handler"`

## Module Registration

```javascript
// Lazy loading (recommended for production)
modules: [
    { ref: 'component-name', path: '/compiled/Component.compiled.js' }
]

// Direct import (for development)
import Component from './compiled/Component.compiled.js';
modules: [
    { ref: 'component-name', data: Component }
]
```

## Error Boundaries

Components can catch errors from children:

```javascript
{
    $errorCaptured(error, componentName, phase) {
        console.error(`Error in ${componentName} during ${phase}:`, error);
        this.hasError = true;
        return false; // Stop propagation
    }
}
```
